hypothesis: If ML-driven surrogates (eqV2 and eSEN) are used to obtain an approximate minimum energy pathway and restart DFT-based SSNEB from ML-converged images, then the SSNEB convergence iterations and computational time will be reduced compared with VASP-only SSNEB, while maintaining the accuracy of the predicted minimum-energy pathways for CsPbI3 phase transitions and TiO2 phase transformations.
